purpose: Deterministic golden cases for method-first Lumen behavior. Expected outputs verify transparency, uncertainty preservation, and visible confidence logic rather than final-truth conclusions.
global_invariants: Do not emit a final truth verdict for an entire article.
Every confidence value must include visible reasons and rule references.
Unknown, unresolved, and contested are valid expected statuses.
Article count alone must not increase source independence.
Anonymous-source claims must be confidence-capped unless stronger evidence is attached.
Narrative analysis must be labeled as analysis, not fact.
Every transformation must be represented by an audit event.
Placeholder extractors must disclose limitations and must not claim production-grade extraction.

cases.input_summary: One article attributes a major security claim to an unnamed official; no document, named witness, or primary record is attached.

claims_under_test.text: An unnamed official said the agency identified the actor responsible for the breach.

cases.input_summary: Ten outlets publish near-identical reports copied from the same wire dispatch.

claims_under_test.text: Multiple reports said the port reopened at noon.

must_not: count ten articles as ten independent confirmations

cases.input_summary: An article says a regulation starts on June 1, but an attached official filing says July 1.

expected.primary_evidence_effect: boost contradiction visibility, not automatic final truth

cases.input_summary: Headline says study proves a cause; body says researchers observed a correlation and warned about confounders.

claims_under_test.text: The study proves the policy caused the change.

must_not: normalize headline hype into proven causality

cases.input_summary: An agency under investigation says its internal review found no wrongdoing; no report is published.

claims_under_test.text: The agency found no wrongdoing in its conduct.

must_not: equate official statement with verified fact

cases.input_summary: Two adversarial organizations and a local reporter independently agree that a meeting occurred, while disagreeing on motive.

claims_under_test.text: The meeting was intended to pressure regulators.

must_not: transfer confidence from occurrence to motive

cases.input_summary: A campaign says incidents doubled, but omits the baseline count, population, time window, and data source.
must_not: present the statistic as meaningful without denominator

cases.input_summary: A post claims a photo is from today; reverse image metadata in the fixture shows the same photo was archived two years earlier.

required_evidence: archive timestamp older than claimed event

must_not: claim the current event did not happen solely because the image is old

cases.input_summary: A breaking report has one vague social post and no named source, document, timestamp, or location.
A deterministic briefing is generated from unresolved claims and local fixture evidence.

claims_under_test.text: The briefing summarizes fixture-backed unresolved claims.

must_not: publish analysis without an audit reference
